summary: Single adults entering DHS shelter nearly doubled between FY22 and FY23 and have stayed far above pre-2022 levels since, driven by the surge of asylum seekers arriving in New York City starting in spring 2022. DHS's own reporting calls FY23 an "unparalleled number of entries" for this population, and even after modest declines in FY24 and FY25, entries remain well above the under-20,000 levels seen in FY21 and FY22.
oneLiner: increasing overall, driven by the asylum-seeker surge that began in 2022
